name: Cangas de Onís, Basílica y Cueva de Covadonga, los lagos Enol y Ercina – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cangas De Onís offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Picos de Europa National Park. The region features rugged peaks, deep valleys, and the Sella River, providing a varied landscape for mountain bikers. Lush valleys and green pastures surround the town, contributing to a mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths. The area's geology creates an environment with significant elevation changes and natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Cangas De Onís The most popular mountain bike trail is Cangas de Onís, Basílica y Cueva de Covadonga, los lagos Enol y Ercina – ruta circular, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route passes by the glacial lakes Enol and Ercina within the Picos de Europa National Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Peruyal, Cangas de Onís, La Cuevona – ruta circular, a difficult 31.6 miles (50.9 km) path. This path offers varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Covadonga Sanctuary – Queen's Viewpoint loop from Cangas de Onís, a 16.7 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through the Picos de Europa National Park, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Cangas De Onís is defined by rugged peaks, the Sella River valley, and lush green pastures. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Cangas De Onís are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cangas De Onís's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cangas De Onís?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Cangas De Onís, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 600 of these routes.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for mountain biking in Cangas De Onís?
A: Mountain biking in Cangas De Onís is generally challenging, with 14 out of 21 routes rated as difficult. There are also 6 moderate trails and 1 easy option, catering to a range of abilities. The terrain often features significant elevation changes due to its location at the foothills of the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cangas De Onís?
A: While many trails in Cangas De Onís are challenging, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. For families looking for a less strenuous ride, it's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot to ensure it matches your family's skill level and preferences. The region's lush valleys and green pastures can offer scenic, gentler sections.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cangas De Onís?
A: You can expect varied terrain, from rugged peaks and deep valleys to lush green pastures and riverbanks along the Sella River. Trails often feature significant elevation changes, with some routes climbing over 1,400 meters, providing both challenging ascents and rewarding descents. The geological formation of the Picos de Europa contributes to this unique and dramatic landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Cangas De Onís?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Picos de Europa National Park. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027483809/cangas-de-onis-basilica-y-cueva-de-covadonga-los-lagos-enol-y-ercina-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas de Onís, Basílica y Cueva de Covadonga, los lagos Enol y Ercina – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; passes by the beautiful glacial Lakes Enol and Ercina. You might also encounter the Sella River, the Beyos Gorge, and various viewpoints. Other notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/492581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covadonga Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/282404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buferrera Mine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cangas De Onís?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Cangas De Onís are circular. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027483809/cangas-de-onis-basilica-y-cueva-de-covadonga-los-lagos-enol-y-ercina-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas de Onís, Basílica y Cueva de Covadonga, los lagos Enol y Ercina – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027483183/la-peruyal-cangas-de-onis-la-cuevona-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Peruyal, Cangas de Onís, La Cuevona – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Cangas de Onís / Cangues d'Onís&lt;/a&gt;. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, often providing diverse scenery along the way.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cangas De Onís?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cangas De Onís are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Picos de Europa, and the challenging yet rewarding nature of the routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cangas De Onís?
A: Cangas de Onís benefits from a mild and humid climate, making it suitable for mountain biking through much of the year. However, the Picos de Europa National Park can experience varying conditions. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring snow to higher elevations, impacting trail accessibility.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints in Cangas De Onís?
A: Absolutely. Many trails provide breathtaking scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Següencu Viewpoint loop from Cangas de Onís / Cangues d'Onís&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you to a prominent viewpoint. The region's rugged peaks and deep valleys naturally offer numerous opportunities for panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological formations accessible near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Cangas de Onís is known for its caves and unique geological features. While mountain biking, you might pass near attractions such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuevona of Cuevas del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7602551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevona de Debodes&lt;/a&gt;. These offer interesting stops to explore the region's underground wonders.

Q: Are there options for food or accommodation along the mountain bike routes?
A: While specific food stops are not detailed for every route, Cangas de Onís itself offers various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. For longer routes, especially those venturing into the Picos de Europa, you might find mountain refuges or huts. For example, the area has several huts like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegabaño Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/574873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Ario Hut&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for planning multi-day trips or longer rides.
tips: The houses of the Indianos have beautiful buildings with a very distinguishable architecture and beautiful colors. The Indians were emigrants who made their fortune in Latin America (Cuba and Mexico, especially). It is also worth seeing the Church of the Holy Cross, the Roman bridge, the Aula del Reino de Asturias, the Parish Church, the Pintu Palace, the Market Square and the Dago House.
It's a beautiful cathedral with very interesting architecture. It was restored after a major fire in the 18th century.

The Santa Cueva de Covadonga is a natural cave and a point of pilgrimage for Catholics, as it is believed to be the place where the Virgin Mary appeared to the Christian army during the Battle of Covadonga in 722. The cave is now a small chapel with a statue of the Virgin Mary in the center.

The views from Mirador de la Reina are absolutely stunning. You can see the surrounding mountains and valleys, as well as the winding road that leads to the viewpoint. Due to its popularity, the viewpoint can get very crowded during the peak tourist season. It is best to arrive early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the crowds.

The best time to visit Lake Enol is in the summer months from July to September, when the weather is pleasant and the lake is not frozen over. The views of the lake and surrounding mountains are breathtaking! The scenery is particularly beautiful early in the morning when the mist rises from the lake. There are picnic areas and restrooms near the lake, as well as a small cafe that sells snacks and drinks.
The second lake of Covadonga that is located above the Enol. The views are spectacular and from the viewpoint you can see the two lakes as well as the Picos de Europa that surround it.
